item.title: FRU CEO says no formal complaints lodged regarding Flying Fijians team environment
item.description: Fiji Rugby Union Chief Executive Officer Koli Sewabu says no formal reports or complaints have been brought to his attention regarding allegations of an unprofessional environment within the Flying Fijians camp. Sewabu made the comments in response to questions from fijivillage News following reports alleging the team environment has been unprofessional, with little planning and organisation, whil
item.content:encoded:
Fiji Rugby Union Chief Executive Officer Koli Sewabu says no formal reports or complaints have been brought to his attention regarding allegations of an unprofessional environment within the Flying Fijians camp.

Sewabu made the comments in response to questions from fijivillage News following reports alleging the team environment has been unprofessional, with little planning and organisation, while players were allegedly being treated more like children than elite athletes.

The reports also claimed inappropriate language and a communication style described by those close to the camp as unprofessional, condescending and unacceptable.

Responding to the allegations, Sewabu says Fiji Rugby has established governance and reporting processes to ensure any concerns relating to player welfare, team management or high-performance operations are raised, assessed and addressed through the appropriate channels.

He says no formal complaint or issue of the nature described has been brought to his attention through those established processes.

Sewabu says it would therefore be inappropriate to comment on or speculate about allegations that have not been formally presented or verified.

He says players, coaches and management all have a responsibility to uphold the values and standards expected of Fiji Rugby, and where genuine concerns exist, they should be raised through the appropriate mechanisms so they can be properly considered.

The FRU CEO says the union has full confidence in its governance structures to deal with any matters professionally, fairly and confidentially, adding that any formal concerns raised will receive the attention and due process they deserve.

As the team prepares to take on Scotland tomorrow morning, Sewabu says the immediate focus remains on the match, with preparations progressing well and the union looking forward to a strong performance.

He also thanked the Fijian community across the United Kingdom for their continued support, saying their encouragement has been a tremendous source of strength for the Flying Fijians.

The Flying Fijians will take on Scotland at 1.10am tomorrow.

In other matches today, the All Blacks will host Ireland at 7.10pm, Japan will take on France at 8.40pm, and the Wallabies will face Italy at 10.10pm.

South Africa will meet Wales at 3.40am, while England will take on Argentina at 7.10am tomorrow.

item.title: Lelean through to two Eastern Zone finals as QVS claims U14 spot
item.description: Lelean Memorial School secured places in two finals after recording two wins in the Vodafone Eastern Zone semi-finals rugby competition. In the Under-14 grade, Queen Victoria School defeated Lelean Memorial School 12-7 at Natovi Ground.
item.content:encoded:
Lelean Memorial School secured places in two finals after recording two wins in the Vodafone Eastern Zone semi-finals rugby competition.

In the Under-14 grade, Queen Victoria School defeated Lelean Memorial School 12-7 at Natovi Ground.

Lelean thrashed Tailevu North College 56-0 in the Under-15 grade at Dawasamu Ground.

In the Under-19 grade, Lelean also defeated Dawasamu Secondary School 15-3 .

In other matches, Ratu Kadavulevu School defeated Tailevu North College 42-0 in the Under-14 grade semi final at St Vincent Ground in Natovi.

RKS also edged Queen Victoria School 19-17 in the Under-15 grade semi final.

Meanwhile, Queen Victoria School defeated Sila Central College 25-11 in the Under-16 grade semi final.

item.title: Nasinu U18 to face Grammar in Southern Zone Deans final after semi-final wins
item.description: The Nasinu Secondary School Under 18 team will take on Suva Grammar High School the final of the Southern Zone Vodafone Deans final next week after they edged Marist Brothers High School 22-17 at the Grammar Grounds today . Grammar thumped Ratu Sukuna Memorial School 24-15 to book a spot in the final.
item.content:encoded:
The Nasinu Secondary School Under 18 team will take on Suva Grammar High School the final of the Southern Zone Vodafone Deans final next week after they edged Marist Brothers High School 22-17 at the Grammar Grounds today .

Grammar thumped Ratu Sukuna Memorial School 24-15 to book a spot in the final.

Grammar continued its dominance in the U17 grade semi final, defeating Naitasiri High School 34-7 while Gospel High School edged Marist15-10.

In the Weet Bix Raluve U16 grade, Grammar beat Lomaivuna Secondary School 15-7 to book a final spot next week.

The finals will be on Saturday at Grammar grounds.

item.title: Vodafone Southern Zone Rugby: Marist and Nasinu set up U15 final while MGM and Nasinu book U16 showdown
item.description: Mikaele Liga , Pita Toganivalu · 18/07/2026 Marist Brothers High School began the Vodafone Southern Zone semi-finals on a high, beating Gospel High School 16-7 in the under-15 grade at the Suva Grammar School grounds.
item.content:encoded:
Mikaele Liga , Pita Toganivalu · 18/07/2026

Marist Brothers High School began the Vodafone Southern Zone semi-finals on a high, beating Gospel High School 16-7 in the under-15 grade at the Suva Grammar School grounds.

Nasinu Secondary School edged Grammar in the other under-15 semi-final 20-17 to book a place in the final against Marist next week.

Suva Grammar School's U16 rugby team lost the semi-final against a spirited Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School 18-11 at the Suva Grammar ground today.

In the other U16 match, Nasinu edged Marist 16-15 to book a place in the final against MGM.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys face hosts PNG in crucial OFC U-16 Championship clash
item.description: The Fiji Junior Bula Boys will face their biggest test of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship when they take on hosts Papua New Guinea today in their final group match. Fiji go into the match with confidence after two impressive victories, defeating New Caledonia 2-0 in their opening game before recording a dominant 5-0 win over Samoa.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Junior Bula Boys will face their biggest test of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship when they take on hosts Papua New Guinea today in their final group match.

Fiji go into the match with confidence after two impressive victories, defeating New Caledonia 2-0 in their opening game before recording a dominant 5-0 win over Samoa.

Head coach Sunil Kumar says Papua New Guinea will not be an easy opponent as they are the hosts and have shown they are a strong side.

He says Fiji must be more clinical in front of goal, and the players must convert their chances when they come.

The Junior Bula Boys will be aiming to continue their unbeaten run and finish the group stage strongly.

Fiji will face Papua New Guinea at 6pm at the PNG Football Stadium in Port Morseby.

The semi-finals of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship will be held next Thursday, while the final will be played next Saturday.

item.title: Flying Fijians put recent defeats behind them ahead of Scotland clash
item.description: More than 51,000 fans are expected to fill Murrayfield Stadium tomorrow as the FIJI Water Flying Fijians prepare to face Scotland, and Fiji players are ready. Flying Fijian Captain Tevita Ikanivere says despite losses to Wales and a heavy defeat to England, the Flying Fijians have put those results behind them and are determined to deliver a strong performance against Scotland.
item.content:encoded:
More than 51,000 fans are expected to fill Murrayfield Stadium tomorrow as the FIJI Water Flying Fijians prepare to face Scotland, and Fiji players are ready.

Flying Fijian Captain Tevita Ikanivere says despite losses to Wales and a heavy defeat to England, the Flying Fijians have put those results behind them and are determined to deliver a strong performance against Scotland.

Ikanivere says the players are fully switched on and ready for the challenge.

The Japan-based hooker says valuable lessons have been learned from their mistakes in the last two matches.

Ikanivere also says the weather conditions are ideal for rugby and the players are mentally prepared to perform in front of a crowd of more than 51,000 supporters.

Fiji will play Scotland at 1.10am tomorrow.

In other matches, the All Blacks will host Ireland at 7.10pm tomorrow, Japan will take on France at 8.40pm, and the Wallabies will face Italy at 10.10pm.

On Sunday, South Africa will meet Wales at 3.40am, while England will take on Argentina at 7.10am.

item.description: Labasa fought back twice to earn a 2-all draw against Suva in an entertaining Extra Premier League clash.Suva got off to a flying

item.description: FIJI Water Flying Fijians Acting Head Coach Senirusi Seruvakula says he is confident the players will deliver a strong performance

item.title: Ba and Navua renew rivalry in crucial Extra Premier League clash
item.description: The rivalry between Ba and Navua will continue when the two sides meet in a highly anticipated Round 14 Extra Premier League clash

item.title: Pelé's 1958 World Cup Final jersey sells for US$4.88 million
item.description: The jersey worn by Brazilian football legend Pelé during the 1958 FIFA World Cup Final has sold for US$4.88 million at an auction
